summary:
Hypertension-induced bleeding primarily affects the basal ganglia region with the highest frequency noted in the putamen. This pattern aligns closely with studies highlighting its role due to increased vascular fragility under high blood pressure conditions. \boxed{\text{Basal Ganglia}} The summary captures the essential point regarding where hypertension typically leads to cerebral bleeds while specifying which part within this region sees more occurrences based on available evidence.
